scenario_name: Tesla Favorability During Musk Twitter Acquisition 2022
description: Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter (Oct 2022) and subsequent controversial actions polarized opinion on Tesla. Democratic favorability of Tesla dropped from ~44% to ~15% while Republican favorability rose. Overall net favorability shifted negative. Tests CEO political polarization contagion to brand.

key_events.description: Musk completes $44B Twitter acquisition (Oct 27, 2022). Immediately fires top executives and begins mass layoffs of ~50% of staff.
Twitter verification chaos, advertiser exodus. Musk tweets increasingly political content, endorses Republicans ahead of midterms.
Musk reinstates banned accounts including Trump, shares conspiracy theories, and promotes anti-trans content. Democratic Tesla favorability drops below 20%.
Tesla stock drops ~65% from peak. Investors worry Musk is distracted. Brand perception surveys show Tesla becoming politically coded.
Morning Consult data shows Tesla's net favorability among Democrats dropped from +44 to -20, while gaining only modestly among Republicans (+15 to +25).

calibration_notes: Tests CEO personal brand contamination of corporate brand, with asymmetric partisan effects. Tesla's core customer base (educated, environmentally conscious, urban) overlapped heavily with demographics most alienated by Musk's post-acquisition behavior. Key dynamics: CEO-brand conflation in personality-driven companies, political polarization contagion, asymmetric brand damage (lost more from alienated base than gained from new supporters), stock price as sentiment amplifier, and the challenge of being simultaneously a liberal product (EVs) and conservative spokesperson.
